$description: Cicrus design tokens — DTCG-style (per-mode values live in $value keyed dark/light/liquid; strict DTCG has no mode axis, so treat the shape as DTCG-compatible, not conformant). Machine-readable mirror of src/tokens.css — the CSS is canonical. Three first-class modes: dark (default), light, liquid.

display.$description: Hero numbers & wordmark. 36px+ only, never body.

heading-weight.$description: Heading weight — light mode needs the heavier cut on paper.

color.$description: Each semantic role resolves to a different primitive per mode (dark / light / liquid). Liquid is Liquid Glass (iOS 26 / macOS Tahoe reference): surfaces are milky white films over whatever sits behind them — OS vibrancy in the native context, an opaque base + painted backdrop in browsers (see src/liquid.css and the glass group). Text alphas composite over the frosted film. Contrast ratios are WCAG 2.1 against the mode's --black.

accent.$description: Signal red. Interrupt only — errors, destructive, the one accent moment per screen. Light keeps brand #D71921 (4.76:1 on paper); dark and liquid brighten it because deep red fails AA as text on OLED black (4.05:1) and on milky films. The interrupt role is identical in all modes.

accent-subtle.$description: Translucent wash of the accent — error-row hover, alert tints. Follows the mode's accent.

error.$description: Alias of accent in every mode (CSS: --error: var(--accent)). Kept for semantic completeness.

warning.$description: Pending, degraded, attention. Light deepens to hold AA on paper (4.55:1).

interactive.$description: Links, info, tappable text — not buttons. AA in every mode (dark 7.0:1, light 5.9:1, liquid 9.6:1 on the panel film).

black.$description: Page background floor. OLED black (dark) / neutral off-white paper (light) / deep blue-black (liquid). In liquid it doubles as ink-on-light (primary-button text) — never redefine it transparent; fix floor occlusions at the usage.

border-visible.$description: Intentional borders, focus, input underline. Non-text: dark 2.8:1 · light 2.5:1 · liquid 3.1:1.

text-disabled.$description: Tertiary meta, placeholders. dark 6.5:1 · light 4.9:1 (4.7:1 on raised surfaces) · liquid 4.8:1 on the panel film — AA everywhere despite the name; safe for meta text.

text-primary.$description: Body copy, data values. dark 17.1:1 · light 14.5:1 · liquid 9.5:1 on the panel film.

ease.$description: Percussive ease-out. No spring, no bounce.

data.$description: Data sweeps: progress fills, bar entrances, gauge arcs. The one duration above view-level — data is allowed to take its time.

glass.$description: Liquid-mode material, consumed by src/liquid.css. iOS 26 / macOS Tahoe reference: the material has NO color of its own — panes are milky white films that frost whatever sits behind them; the backdrop supplies the color, the material supplies the frost. Two rendering contexts: native (body.liquid.native — OS vibrancy behind a faint veil) and browser (opaque base + painted backdrop as fallback). The white tint matters more than the blur; saturate(200%) keeps backdrop color glowing through. CONTRAST CAVEAT: text alphas clear AA over the panel film on the browser backdrop; in the native context the OS desktop is unbounded — over a white desktop the film+veil stack collapses below AA. Host apps own their backdrop: keep it mid-tone or darker, or add a contrast plate behind text-dense panes.

panel.$description: Cards, panels, long-form blocks — the standalone-surface material.

raised.$description: True surfaces: inputs, selects, chips, thumbnails, nested panes. No blur.

heavy.$description: Modals — must occlude what they float over; pairs with the shared blur.

veil.$description: Native context: the only thing the page paints; OS vibrancy supplies the rest.

blur.$description: One blur for all glass; one pass per stack.

card.$description: Liquid is the one mode that softens radius; dark/light keep 8px/4px.

fallback.$description: All glass surfaces when backdrop-filter is unsupported.

shadow.$description: Mode-aware shadows. Dark mode draws with light, not shadow — both default to none there.

card.$description: Card resting shadow. Liquid composes rim+shadow in liquid.css instead.
